item.title: Thoughts
item.content:encoded:
When I read the containment procedures and description of this object I really didn't like it. I thought that the concept was really boring "Just a diving suit that drowns people on land". However, I really like the mystery that you set up in the addendums. It made me think "Where did they come from?" and "Why are they in landlocked countries?".

You should add an incident report or two where agents find these things or their victims in strange places. Like if an agent investigated a missing persons case and walks into that missing person's attic. When he opens the attic copious amounts of water just pour out and he finds a blue, drowned corpse in the attic. That would really round it out. It'd be really cool to see another related SCP that explains their origins and objective. You got my vote!

item.title: Re: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Ok thank you for the info, I’ll do that as soon as possible once I have access to my computer on Sunday.

UPDATE: Got it done

item.title: Re: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Images used are public domain/marked for reuse and were found on pixabay.

You're going to need a direct link to the image information so staff can confirm that it adheres to the site's Creative Commons license.

item.title: Re: Credits
item.content:encoded:
This is much better than the orginal draft that you had.
Good Job mate.

item.title: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Huge thanks to Doctor North on the SCP Discord for aiding me in development of this article.

Images used are public domain/marked for reuse and were found on pixabay.

SCP-4253 - https://bit.ly/2GWsHvD
SCP-4253-B - https://bit.ly/2tQcIqC
